summary: Latvia's posture is carried by CERT.LV, the national computer security incident response team, which engages with both post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution and frames quantum-safe infrastructure within the European EuroQCI initiative. Its Q-Ready conference brings together work on quantum-safe security, but no national migration timeline or standards selection has been published.
govActors: CERT.LV | Latvian national computer security incident response team, hosts the Q-Ready conference and events on quantum-safe cryptography
provenance: CERT.LV lead, PQC and QKD, EuroQCI framing: Q-Ready 25 conference announcement (ingested)

Survey of PQC methods (lattice-based NTRU/LWE, code-based McEliece, multivariate): presentation hosted on CERT.LV events area, authored by an academic researcher rather than CERT.LV, flagged for review (ingested, T4)
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp (NIST/ETSI/ISO) named as national guidance in captured summaries
algorithms families named only inside an academic survey, not adopted as Latvian guidance, so standardFamilies/algorithms omitted - REVIEW
hybridDeployment/migrationTimeline/targetCompletion omitted: not stated in captured summaries
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / taker, High): EU coordinated roadmap (NIS Cooperation Group); adopts the NIST baseline as a taker. No national algorithm.
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap

mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on PQC | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2025) | EU | soft-law
obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2; migration follows the EU coordinated 2026/2030/2035 timeline. No distinct national post-quantum instrument is recorded in the Atlas sources.
analyticalNote: Latvia is bound by the EU regulatory spine (NIS2, DORA) and follows the coordinated roadmap; no national algorithm or dedicated post-quantum statute is recorded, so it adopts the NIST baseline under EU coordination. Watch point: national transposition and any country-specific guidance would refine this EU-floor entry.
